summary.intent: Address round-4 Codex PR review findings for the color_picker component, implementing 5 fixes across disabled guard, format select, channel dispatcher, root attrs, and color-space labels.
summary.outcome: All 5 findings implemented with named tests, spec synced (§1.7/1.8/2/3.1), 103 tests passing, clippy clean, coverage gate passed, and changes committed and pushed.

repo: Color-space labels should be user-facing strings (e.g. 'RGB', 'HSL') not debug/enum forms (e.g. 'Rgb')
Spec lives at spec/components/specialized/color-picker.md and must be kept in sync with implementation; anatomy tables, ARIA tables, and prose sections (§1.7, §1.8, §2, §3.1) all require updates
Spec has a validate + snippet-compile gate that must pass before committing
Snapshot tests for root variants (closed/open/disabled/readonly) automatically pick up new HTML attributes like `dir`
The disabled guard pattern requires explicit allowlisting of messages (e.g. SetEyedropperSupported) that must pass through even when disabled
insta orphan check is part of the test suite gate

code.finding: SetEyedropperSupported must be explicitly allowed through the disabled guard; format_select_attrs must propagate the disabled state; set_channel dispatcher added as public API with display-unit round-tripping; root_attrs gains dir via self.ctx.dir.as_html_attr(); color_space_label helper added near channel_token

workflow: Round-trip: implement fixes → compile → run tests → fix test assertions → re-accept snapshots → clippy → spec sync → validate spec → coverage gate → commit/push → reply/resolve PR threads → re-trigger Codex review
Coverage gate is run as a background task (ScheduleWakeup) and awaited before committing
Snapshot updates for structural changes (adding `dir` attribute) are intentional and expected — re-accept with insta

friction: Clippy required a follow-up fix after initial round-4 implementation — clean compile does not guarantee clean clippy
Anatomy tables required manual realignment after edits
Multiple spec sections (§1.7, §1.8, §2, §3.1) needed independent targeted edits — no single update point for cross-cutting changes
open_items: Re-trigger @codex review after push to get round-5 feedback
Reply and resolve the 5 round-4 PR threads (mentioned as next step after coverage gate)
